one_time_description: Germyn Sok Settlement with Canada for $75,000,000, subsequently allocated entirely to membership
economic_notes: Subsequent to year end, First Nation entered agreement to purchase shares of Widsten Holdings Inc. for $12,700,000, which controls a subsidiary operating the Shearwater Marine location. COVID-19 pandemic noted as potential operational risk.
notes: Fiscal year is 2021 (ended March 31, 2021). Large one-time $75M Germyn Sok settlement recorded in 2020 comparative year but fully allocated to membership resulting in nil net impact. 2021 includes large deferred revenue balance of $72.9M primarily from Government of Canada ($39.1M) and BC Reconciliation Funding ($22.3M). total_government_transfers calculated as ISC $16,755,799 + Gov of Canada $40,809,991 + CMHC $5,645,000 + Province of BC $3,238,458 + BC Housing $2,612,606 + DFO $1,296,815 + Coast Conservation Endowment $650,000 + BC FN Gaming $1,133,149 - deferred revenue adjustment offset = approximate; osr calculated as total revenue minus identified government transfers. No schedule of remuneration provided. Subsequent event: purchase of Widsten Holdings Inc. for $12.7M post year-end. num_gbes = 3 (Heiltsuk Holdings LP, Heiltsuk Holdings Ltd., Central Coast Commercial Fisheries LP). num_subsidiaries = 5 fully consolidated entities.
